narrative: OMX30 faces a mixed macro picture on Tuesday morning: Asia's momentum (+3.26% Nikkei, +1.79% Shanghai) and commodity tailwinds (gold +1.65%, copper +2.97%) provide support, while the US close turned negative (S&P -0.19%, Dow -0.59%) and US 10Y yields spiked +125bps to 4.598%, creating DCF headwinds for growth and real estate. Technically, the index remains in a bull regime (+4.8% vs 200-MA) but sits -0.3% below the 50-MA with stochastic indicators deeply oversold (K=7.8, D=13.9), suggesting near-term mean reversion potential; however, ADX at 40.9 with bearish DI bias indicates strong selling pressure offsetting this. Positioning near the lower Bollinger band (%B=0.15) and -3.7% from the 52-week high reflects recent consolidation. The weighted correlation signal (+0.221%) and narrowing VIX (-5.68% to 17.59) suggest a patience-testing sideways session rather than a directional breakout.
morningBrief: OMX30 opens Tuesday in a holding pattern: Asia's overnight strength and commodity gains (copper +2.97% is key for Boliden) compete against US rate shock and equity weakness. Key technical level to watch is 3123 (yesterday's close); break below 3085 (lower Bollinger) risks a test of the 50-MA around 3150. Stochastic oversold reading could prompt short-covering rallies, but ADX 40.9 with strong bearish DI warns not to chase longs aggressively. Sector focus: Banks (SEB, Handelsbanken) benefit from higher US 10Y yields widening NIM, but Real Estate (Balder, Castellum) faces refinancing headwinds. Tech/Telecom (Ericsson, Hexagon) and growth names struggle with yield move. Watch Boliden on copper strength and any follow-through from Asia's overnight risk-on. UK fiscal uncertainty (Burnham/Healey/Dimon headlines) adds micro-cap noise. US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET could catalyze volatility; remain tactical until clearer directional signal emerges.
drivers: Asian strength (Nikkei +3.26%, Shanghai +1.79%) offsetting US weakness
Commodity support: Gold +1.65%, Copper +2.97% benefit mining/materials
US yields spike (+1.25%) headwind for real estate and tech valuations
Stochastic oversold (K=7.8) suggests mean reversion setup but ADX 40.9 shows strong directional bias
UK policy uncertainty (Burnham/Healey fiscal plans) creates cross-border noise

sectorImpacts.reason: US 10Y yield surge to 4.598% (+125bps) expands NIM expectations; Swedish banks with USD/EUR funding benefit from steeper curves.
Rising yields increase discount rates and refinancing costs for property portfolios; real estate valuations compress in rate-up environment.
NASDAQ -0.05% overnight, US yields up sharply — growth-heavy valuations face DCF compression; sentiment weak post-US close.
Gold +1.65% and copper +2.97% provide direct revenue lift; Boliden is primary beneficiary of copper strength.
Brent oil -0.76% eases input cost pressure; EUR/SEK -0.07% and USD/SEK -0.05% offer minimal export FX benefit today. Mixed signals.
Rising rates create consumer spending headwind; Brent oil weakness and higher financing costs pressure discretionary demand.

watchList.reason: Copper +2.97% and gold +1.65% overnight provide significant revenue tailwinds; direct commodity exposure makes this the prime beneficiary.
watchList.catalyst: Commodities strength; if copper sustains above 6.45, consider tactical accumulation.

watchList.reason: US 10Y yield +125bps to 4.598% expands net interest margin; Swedish banking sector benefits from rate steepness.
watchList.catalyst: Yields; watch for further ECB guidance if rates continue higher.

watchList.reason: NASDAQ -0.05%, US yields crush growth valuations; tech sentiment weak. Oversold stochastic may offer tactical bounce, but trend is down.
watchList.catalyst: Tech sector rotation; if NASDAQ stabilizes, re-evaluate.

watchList.reason: EUR/SEK -0.07% is minimal headwind; rising rates and risk-off sentiment weigh on consumer discretionary. Watch for any policy clarity on UK sourcing.
watchList.catalyst: Retail sales data, consumer confidence, yield direction.

watchList.reason: Real estate REIT facing sharp refinancing cost increase; yield surge to 4.598% compresses property valuations materially.
watchList.catalyst: Rate expectations; hold short until ECB signals pause.

econCalendarAlert: No major Swedish economic releases today. US Jobless Claims (initial + continuing) at 14:30 CET is the key catalyst; any surprise (hotter = bearish for OMX, colder = relief rally). Watch for any ECB policymaker commentary on rate path.
topRisk: US yields continue spiking (10Y already +125bps) — if 4.6% level breaks higher, could trigger a sharp rotation out of growth/real estate and compress equity multiples further. Secondary risk: UK fiscal announcements (Burnham/Healey) create headlines but are micro for OMX; JPMorgan/Dimon tax warning may spill into sentiment.
technicalSummary: Bull regime intact (+4.8% vs 200-MA) but -0.3% vs 50-MA signals consolidation
RSI 39.21 neutral; stochastic K=7.8 deeply oversold (D=13.9) — potential mean reversion trigger
MACD below signal with hist=-9.569 shows downside momentum; ADX 40.9 bearish DI bias confirms strong selling pressure
Bollinger %B=0.15 in lower half; -3.7% from 52W high reflects recent distribution
No extreme oversold RSI risk (not below 30), but stochastic setup warrants caution on shorts
